label: Do you prefer bedroom doors open or closed?
I need daily alone time at home to recharge.

bipolarLabels.left: I leave my monitors/papers set up permanently
bipolarLabels.right: I completely pack away all work items every evening

label: If you work from home using the shared dining/living room table, how do you manage your equipment at the end of the workday?
Common areas for socializing, bedrooms for privacy.
Quiet reading in living room while others chat softly is okay.
What is your default policy on housemates borrowing everyday household items (e.g., phone chargers, a splash of milk, basic tools) without asking first?

options.label: Strictly ask first, every single time
Take it if it's an emergency, but tell me immediately after
What's mine is yours; no need to ask for basics

label: I want my own fridge/pantry shelf clearly marked.
Comfortable pooling spices/condiments in a shared bin.
If you own expensive kitchen equipment (e.g., a Le Creuset pan or high-end Japanese knives), do you allow your housemate to use them?

options.label: No, they are strictly for my personal use

label: When you are home and awake in your bedroom, what is the default state of your door?
I would be uncomfortable if my housemate placed an always-listening smart speaker (e.g., Amazon Echo, Google Home) in the shared living room or kitchen.
I expect my housemate to ask for my explicit permission before posting any photos or videos on social media that show the inside of our shared apartment.
Quiet calls in living room during day are okay.
Do you intend to store large personal equipment (e.g., an expensive road bike, golf clubs, large musical instruments) inside the shared living areas rather than a basement or shed?

bipolarLabels.left: Highly dynamic; anyone can move furniture or add art at any time
bipolarLabels.right: Highly static; no changes should be made without a joint discussion

label: How do you view the layout and decoration of the shared living room?
If I go on a 3-week vacation or work trip, I believe it is okay to temporarily sublet my room to cover my rent.
Prefer decor in common areas discussed first.

bipolarLabels.left: Highly relaxed (e.g., walking around in underwear/towel is fine)
bipolarLabels.right: Highly modest (e.g., always fully dressed or in a thick robe)

label: What is your standard for visual modesty when walking between the bathroom and your bedroom in the mornings or evenings?
Comfortable with shared parcel handling (sign/collect).
I am completely comfortable with my housemate installing a video doorbell (e.g., Ring) or an indoor security camera pointing at the front door.
How do you prefer to manage personal toiletries (shampoo, skincare, toothbrush) in a shared bathroom?

options.label: Keep everything stored in the bathroom on my dedicated shelf
Leave a few basics in the shower, but store personal items in my room
Use a shower caddy and bring everything back to my room after every use

label: I require my housemate to notify me immediately if the landlord, a plumber, or any maintenance worker is scheduled to enter the apartment, even if I am not home.
If my bedroom door is closed, I expect my housemate to knock/text me and explicitly wait for a verbal invitation before opening the door, even in urgent situations.

bipolarLabels.left: Fluid; items can overflow into other shelves if needed
bipolarLabels.right: Rigid; crossing into my assigned shelf is a violation of space

label: How strictly do you enforce the boundaries of assigned shelves in the refrigerator or pantry?
I want clearly defined personal zones in the living room (shelves/desk).
